name: Shrutakarma
description: Shrutakarma is a significant figure in Hindu mythology, primarily known as the son of Sahadeva, one of the Pandava brothers in the epic Mahabharata, and his wife, Vijaya. He was a valiant warrior, embodying the qualities of courage, skill, and loyalty characteristic of the Pandava lineage. Shrutakarma played an active role in the Kurukshetra War, fighting on the side of the Pandavas against the Kauravas. While not as prominently featured as some of the other major heroes, his contributions to the Pandava victory were undoubtedly valuable. He was known for his prowess with various weapons, particularly the mace and the sword, and his strategic acumen on the battlefield. His lineage and association with Sahadeva, known for his wisdom and justice, further highlight Shrutakarma's virtuous character. He met his end during the Kurukshetra War, displaying unwavering bravery until his final moments. Shrutakarma's life serves as an example of dharma and dedication to one's duty, even in the face of overwhelming odds. His story, though not as widely recounted, is an integral part of the larger narrative of the Mahabharata, enriching the epic's tapestry of heroism and sacrifice.
subtitle: Valiant son of Sahadeva.
achievements: Fought valiantly in the Kurukshetra War
Demonstrated skill in wielding various weapons
Upheld Dharma and loyalty to the Pandava cause
Displayed courage in the face of death

keyMyths.title: Birth and Lineage
keyMyths.description: Shrutakarma was born to Sahadeva and his wife, Vijaya. He belonged to the Pandava lineage, inheriting their values of righteousness and duty.

keyMyths.title: Role in Kurukshetra War
keyMyths.description: Shrutakarma actively participated in the Kurukshetra War, fighting alongside his father and uncles. He contributed to the Pandava effort, displaying skill and bravery on the battlefield.

keyMyths.title: Death in Battle
keyMyths.description: Shrutakarma met his end during the Kurukshetra War, fighting valiantly against the Kauravas. His death is a testament to his dedication to dharma and his unwavering loyalty.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the life and deeds of Shrutakarma.
sources.text: The primary source for the life and deeds of Shrutakarma.
